title: Wales: Arias above Harlech, Rhinog & Porthmadog

main_route.excerpt: We stopped at the camping shop beyond Wolverhampton for a bigger dog harness & a second green chair.
Lunch was a good site — Llanfaircaerynnan — a train station.
3.50. we arrived at [?Arias], above Harlech [?Llanbedr]. The 6 boys went up for a long walk in the hills.
We arrived home via Smethwick at 4.0.

excursions.label: Sunday church at Dyffryn Ardudwy area

stops.excerpt: Church at [?Dyffrynesthaddu] at 11.30. Trinity Sunday. Half the prayer book is in Welsh.
We left at 10 to Rhinog. Old drovers' roads, ladder stiles. Bog, old paths, lovely views of the sea.
We set off for Harlech & looked at the castle.
We drove on to Porthmadoc to see Erik Scott's slate factory - purple, grey, green slate. We had fish & chips & wandered around the town.

unresolved.issue: Campsite name uncertain; the diary transcription reads '[?Arias] above Harlech [?Llanbedr]'. Could be a farm or campsite name near Llanbedr, Gwynedd. Location anchored approximately at Llanbedr.
Church location uncertain; could be Dyffryn Ardudwy (village near Harlech with a church) or another 'Dyffryn' settlement in the Harlech area. Transcription unclear.
unresolved.excerpt: Church at [?Dyffrynesthaddu] at 11.30. Trinity Sunday. Half the prayer book is in Welsh.

unresolved.issue: Probable Llanfair Caereinion (terminus of the Welshpool & Llanfair Light Railway, matching 'a train station'), but spelling is uncertain. Could also be a different Llanfair- settlement.
unresolved.excerpt: Lunch was a good site — Llanfaircaerynnan — a train station.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: preceding content on A_0199_R (Calke Abbey visit) belongs to the prior trip (1997-derbyshire-calke-abbey-day-trip); main trip narrative begins at 'Saturday 24th May' heading.
summary: A Whitsun bank-holiday camping trip to a hillside campsite above Harlech in North Wales, with the family (plus French guest Geoffrey de Pamafieu and dog Tinder) based for five nights near Llanbedr. Day excursions included a seven-mile walk in the Rhinog hills, a visit to Harlech Castle, and a trip to Porthmadog to see a slate factory.
extraction_notes: The campsite name '[?Arias]' is uncertain and may be a farm or site name. '[?Dyffrynesthaddu]' (Sunday church) is probably Dyffryn Ardudwy. 'Llanfaircaerynnan' (outbound lunch stop) is probably Llanfair Caereinion. The Calke Abbey content at the top of A_0199_R is from the preceding day-trip entry (mixed_start=true) and is excluded from this trip's route. Dates confirmed: 27 May 1997 was indeed a Tuesday. No ferry crossings — entirely within Great Britain.
